perturbation.original_text: To the extent that the transfer or assignment of any SpinCo IP Asset or the assumption of any SpinCo IP Liability requires Approvals or Notifications, the Parties shall use their commercially reasonable efforts to obtain or make such Approvals or Notifications as soon as reasonably practicable; provided, however, that, except to the extent expressly provided in this Agreement or as otherwise agreed in writing between Parent and SpinCo, neither Parent nor SpinCo shall be obligated to contribute capital or pay any consideration in any form (including providing any letter of credit, guaranty or other financial accommodation) to any Person in order to obtain or make such Approvals or Notifications.
perturbation.changed_text: To the extent that the transfer or assignment of any SpinCo IP Asset or the assumption of any SpinCo IP Liability requires Approvals or Notifications, the Parties shall consider using efforts to obtain or make such Approvals or Notifications as they deem fit; provided, however, that, except to the extent expressly provided in this Agreement or as otherwise agreed in writing between Parent and SpinCo, neither Parent nor SpinCo shall be obligated to contribute capital or pay any consideration in any form (including providing any letter of credit, guaranty or other financial accommodation) to any Person in order to obtain or make such Approvals or Notifications.
perturbation.explanation: The original text requires 'commercially reasonable efforts' which is a defined legal standard. The modified text introduces ambiguity by stating the parties shall 'consider using efforts... as they deem fit.' This makes the effort discretionary rather than obligatory, creating a loophole for non-compliance.
perturbation.law_explanation: Section 141(a) states that 'The business and affairs of every corporation organized under this chapter shall be managed by or under the direction of a board of directors'. By introducing ambiguity that allows parties to arbitrarily decide whether to obtain necessary approvals, the modified text undermines the board's responsibility to ensure compliance with laws and regulations, effectively contradicting this section of Delaware law.
perturbation.scraped_snippet_1: Delaware Code Online Title 8 > Chapter 1 Authenticated PDF Â§ 141 Â§ 142 Â§ 143 Â§ 144 Â§ 145 Â§ 146 Â§ 147 TITLE 8 Corporations CHAPTER 1. General Corporation Law Subchapter IV. Directors and Officers Â§ 141. Board of directors; powers; number, qualifications, terms and quorum; committees; classes of directors; nonstock corporations; reliance upon books; action without meeting; removal. (a) The business and affairs of every corporation organized under this chapter shall be managed by or under the direction of a board of directors, except as may be otherwise provided in this chapter or in its certificate of incorporation. If any such provision is made in the certificate of incorporation, the powers and duties conferred or imposed upon the board of directors by this chapter shall be exercised or performed to such extent and by such person or persons as shall be provided in the certificate of incorporation. (b) The board of directors of a corporation shall consist of 1 or more members, each of whom shall be a natural person. The number of directors shall be fixed by, or in the manner provided in, the bylaws, unless the certificate of incorporation fixes the number of directors, in which case a change in the number of directors shall be made only by amendment of the certificate. Directors need not be stockholders unless so required by the certificate of incorporation or the bylaws. The certificate of incorporation or bylaws may prescribe other qualifications for directors. Each director shall hold office until such directorâs successor is elected and qualified or until such directorâs earlier resignation or removal. Any director may resign at any time upon notice given in writing or by electronic transmission to the corporation. A resignation is effective when the resignation is delivered unless the resignation specifies a later effective date or an effective date determined upon the happening of an event or events. A resignation which is conditioned upon the director failing to receive a specified vote for reelection as a direc
perturbation.justification: The original contract requires 'commercially reasonable efforts' to obtain approvals, establishing an obligatory standard. The changed text replaces this with 'consider using efforts... as they deem fit,' making the effort discretionary and creating ambiguity regarding the parties' obligations. While the Law Explanation references Section 141(a) and the board's responsibility, a more direct contradiction arises from the weakening of the obligation itself. Scraped Law Snippet 1 discusses the powers and duties of the board, reinforcing the idea that the board has responsibility. By making the effort discretionary, the changed text allows for the possibility that no efforts are made, undermining the original obligation and creating a loophole. This is a Ambiguities Legal Contradiction as stated in the Perturbation Definition.

perturbation.original_text: Each of Parent and SpinCo, at the request of the other, shall use its commercially reasonable efforts to obtain, or to cause to be obtained, as soon as reasonably practicable, any consent, substitution, approval or amendment required to novate or assign all SpinCo IP Liabilities and obtain in writing the unconditional release of each member of the Parent Group that is a party to any such arrangements, so that, in any such case, the members of the SpinCo Group shall be solely responsible for such SpinCo IP Liabilities; provided, however, that, except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement or any of the Ancillary Agreements, neither Parent nor SpinCo shall be obligated to contribute any capital or pay any consideration in any form (including providing any letter of credit, guaranty or other financial accommodation) to any third Person from whom any such consent, substitution, approval, amendment or release is requested.
perturbation.changed_text: Each of Parent and SpinCo, at the request of the other, may attempt to obtain, or to cause to be obtained, any consent, substitution, approval or amendment required to novate or assign all SpinCo IP Liabilities and obtain in writing the unconditional release of each member of the Parent Group that is a party to any such arrangements, so that, in any such case, the members of the SpinCo Group shall be solely responsible for such SpinCo IP Liabilities; provided, however, that, except as otherwise expressly provided in this Agreement or any of the Ancillary Agreements, neither Parent nor SpinCo shall be obligated to contribute any capital or pay any consideration in any form (including providing any letter of credit, guaranty or other financial accommodation) to any third Person from whom any such consent, substitution, approval, amendment or release is requested.
perturbation.explanation: By changing 'shall use its commercially reasonable efforts' to 'may attempt to obtain', the obligation to obtain necessary consents and approvals becomes optional. This introduces ambiguity, as it is unclear what constitutes an 'attempt' and whether any actual effort is required. This creates a legal contradiction.
perturbation.law_explanation: By making the effort to obtain necessary consents for the transfer of IP liabilities optional rather than obligatory, the modified text contradicts the standard of care required by directors to manage the corporation's affairs in compliance with the law. Section 141 places that responsibility on the board - this change allows them to abdicate that responsibility.

A resignation which is conditioned upon the director failing to receive a specified vote for reelection as a direc
perturbation.justification: The change from 'shall use its commercially reasonable efforts' to 'may attempt to obtain' directly undermines the obligation to secure necessary consents. While the law snippet discusses the board's responsibility to manage corporate affairs, the core contradiction lies in weakening a mandatory action to an optional one. The original text imposes a duty; the changed text introduces ambiguity and weakens this duty, creating a contradiction as compliance becomes uncertain. This aligns with the 'Ambiguities' perturbation definition because the obligation becomes difficult to enforce.

perturbation.original_text: Without limiting the foregoing, prior to, on and after the Effective Time, each Party hereto shall cooperate with the other Party, and without any further consideration, but at the expense of the requesting Party, to execute and deliver, or use its reasonable best efforts to cause to be executed and delivered, all instruments, including instruments of conveyance, assignment and transfer, and to make all filings with, and to obtain all Approvals or Notifications of, any Governmental Authority or any other Person under any permit, license, agreement, indenture or other instrument (including any consents or Governmental Approvals), and to take all such other actions as such Party may reasonably be requested to take by the other Party from time to time, consistent with the terms of this Agreement, in order to effectuate the provisions and purposes of this Agreement and the transfers of the SpinCo IP Assets and the assignment and assumption of the SpinCo IP Liabilities and the other transactions contemplated hereby and thereby.
perturbation.changed_text: Without limiting the foregoing, prior to, on and after the Effective Time, each Party hereto shall consider cooperating with the other Party, and without any further consideration, but at the expense of the requesting Party, to execute and deliver, or consider using its best efforts to cause to be executed and delivered, all instruments, including instruments of conveyance, assignment and transfer, and to consider making all filings with, and to consider obtaining all Approvals or Notifications of, any Governmental Authority or any other Person under any permit, license, agreement, indenture or other instrument (including any consents or Governmental Approvals), and to consider taking all such other actions as such Party may reasonably be requested to take by the other Party from time to time, consistent with the terms of this Agreement, in order to effectuate the provisions and purposes of this Agreement and the transfers of the SpinCo IP Assets and the assignment and assumption of the SpinCo IP Liabilities and the other transactions contemplated hereby and thereby.
perturbation.explanation: The repeated use of 'consider' before key actions (cooperating, using best efforts, making filings, obtaining approvals) transforms mandatory obligations into discretionary ones. This creates significant ambiguity about the extent to which the parties are actually bound to perform these actions.
perturbation.law_explanation: Delaware law, specifically § 141 regarding the powers of the board of directors, requires diligent management of the corporation. The insertion of 'consider' introduces significant ambiguity and undermines the mandatory nature of actions required to transfer assets and liabilities, which could result in a failure to diligently manage the business and affairs of the company, therefore the Delaware code is contradicted.

A resignation which is conditioned upon the director failing to receive a specified vote for reelection as a direc
perturbation.justification: The original text mandates cooperation and actions to effectuate the agreement, while the changed text replaces these mandates with 'consider cooperating,' 'consider using best efforts,' etc. This weakens the obligations and creates ambiguity about whether the parties are actually required to perform these actions, potentially undermining the purpose of the agreement. Scraped Law Snippet 1, particularly section (a), states that 'the business and affairs of every corporation...shall be managed by or under the direction of a board of directors...' By making the cooperation discretionary instead of mandatory, the changed text contradicts the diligent management expected by Delaware law, because it makes compliance and action uncertain.
